ActionConfig.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

AwarenessKind.description: What a placement marker on the awareness/security layer watches for.

AwarenessMarker.description:
A placement point for the awareness/security layer (feature flag
"awarenessLayer", see src/features.ts): a motion marker pings a ripple
where its sensor fires; a safety marker (leak, smoke, door left open)
blinks in an alert palette when its sensor trips. One entity per marker;
`kind` selects which visual and which trip rule apply â€” see
isMarkerTripped in awareness.ts.

FeaturesConfig.description:
Optional feature toggles. Every flag defaults to off; enabling one turns on
a layer, interaction, or editor affordance that is otherwise inert. See
`FEATURE_META` in `features.ts` for user-facing labels/descriptions.

autoPopulateArea.description: Editor: drop an HA area's entities into a room.

backgroundTrace.description: Editor: trace over a blueprint image.

dayNightTheme.description: Dim the plan with the sun.

energyLayer.description: Colour devices by live power draw.

lightsLayer.description: Tint rooms/lamps to a bulb's real colour and brightness.

mediaNowPlaying.description: Show a now-playing cue on TVs/speakers.

roomTapScenes.description: Tap a room to run a scene or toggle its lights.

Floor.description:
A single floor/level. Each floor owns its own set of elements. The canvas
size, grid and background are shared across floors (config-level).

awareness.description:
Awareness/security markers (feature flag "awarenessLayer"): motion pings
and safety alerts. Optional, like `rooms` â€” an older config simply has
none, and normalizeFloor deliberately does not backfill an empty array
here (see the `rooms` handling in normalizeFloor, unchanged by this).

haFloor.description:
Optional link to a Home Assistant floor (its registry `floor_id`).
Selecting one in the editor names this floor after it; the id is kept so
future features (e.g. area filtering, per-floor entity defaults) can use
the association. Purely additive â€” nothing renders differently today.

image.description:
Optional background image URL (e.g. `/local/floorplan.png` or an external
URL) drawn behind the elements â€” handy for tracing over a real floor plan.
It fills the virtual canvas, so match the canvas width/height to the image
aspect ratio to avoid distortion.

imageLocked.description:
Editor-only: when true and the `backgroundTrace` feature is on, the
image URL and opacity controls are disabled in the editor so they can't
be changed by accident while tracing. Has no effect on the live card â€”
`floorplan-card.ts` never reads this field.

rooms.description: Room polygons, drawn beneath the walls. Optional: older configs have none.

rotation.description:
Display-only quarter-turn rotation. Coordinates are always canonical; this
rotates the drawn plan (and swaps the stage footprint at 90/270). The editor
edits unrotated and preserves this field untouched.

display.description: How the device is drawn. Default "badge".

double_tap_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

entity.description:
The entity this item reflects. Optional: a device with no entity in Home
Assistant still belongs on the plan as a static marker â€” it draws its icon,
shows no state, and performs no action.

hold_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

icon.description: Optional override icon (mdi:...). Falls back to the entity's icon.

name.description: Optional label override. Falls back to the entity's friendly name.

powerEntity.description: A power/energy sensor (watts) whose live value colours this item in the energy layer.

rippleColor.description: Ripple ring color (CSS/hex). Falls back to the primary color.

rippleSize.description: Max ripple ring diameter in pixels. Default 80.

secondaryEntity.description:
Optional second entity (e.g. a humidity sensor paired with a temperature
entity). When set and the state is shown, both values are displayed in the
same element. The primary `entity` drives on/off state and click actions.

showIcon.description: Show the icon badge. When false only the state/label shows. Default true.

showState.description: Show the entity state next to the icon.

stateStyles.description:
Conditional styling, first match wins. Rules do not stack: a cascade of
partial styles is how every conditional-formatting system becomes
unexplainable.

tap_action.description: Lovelace actions. Defaults: tap = toggle (controllable domains) or more-info; hold/double = none.

FloorText.description: A free text label placed on the plan.

color.description: Text color (CSS color / hex). Falls back to the theme text color.

Furniture.description: A gray furniture/fixture diagram placed on the plan.

color.description: Stroke/fill color. Defaults to gray so it reads differently from walls.

double_tap_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

entity.description: Optional entity. With one, the piece is a live, controllable appliance.

hand.description: L-shaped sectional only: which side the chaise extends on. Default `right`.

hold_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

showState.description: Show the entity's state text on the piece.

stateStyles.description: Conditional colour/animation/icon, first match wins (same engine as rooms/items).

tap_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

Opening.description:
A door or window. Positioned by its center point and rotation so it can be
dropped onto (and aligned with) a wall, but it is stored independently.

activeColor.description: Color of the leaf/sash and swing arc while actively open. Falls back to the primary color.

doorStyle.description:
Swing doors only: leaf arrangement. "double" draws two half-width leaves,
one hinged at each jamb, meeting at the centre (French doors). Ignored for
windows and for non-swing motions.

entity.description:
Optional entity (e.g. a contact `binary_sensor` or a `cover`) whose state
drives whether the opening is drawn open or closed. When unset, doors are
drawn open (swing symbol) and windows closed, matching a static floor plan.

flipH.description:
Mirror the symbol leftâ†”right in the opening's local frame. For a swing door
this moves the hinge to the other jamb; for a slider it reverses the slide
direction. Absent = the default orientation (hinge/anchor at the left jamb).

flipV.description:
Mirror the symbol across the wall line, so the door opens into the room on
the other side. Absent = the default (swings toward the âˆ’y / "near" side).

foldPanels.description:
Folding openings only ("fold"): number of equal leaves in the hinge chain.
Must be even so the free end rides the track; 2 (default) or 4.

invert.description: Flip the open/closed interpretation of `entity` (for inverted sensors).

length.description: Length along the wall, in virtual units.

motion.description:
How the opening moves. `swing` (default) is a hinged door / casement window;
`slide` is a sliding door / sliding window whose panel(s) travel along the
wall (see {@link sliderStyle}). New: `roll` (sectional/garage) and `fold`
(bi-fold).

sliderStyle.description:
Sliding openings only (`motion: "slide"`): how the panels are arranged.
- `single` (default) â€” one panel slides aside into the wall.
- `bypass` â€” two panels on parallel tracks; one slides behind the other
  (patio-door style).
- `biparting` â€” two panels meet in the middle and part, each recessing into
  the wall on its own side.
Ignored for swinging openings.

type.description: The kind of opening: a `door` (single leaf) or a `window` (two leaves / glass).

Room.description:
A room: a closed polygon drawn beneath everything else.

Deliberately **not** derived from the walls. Deriving rooms means planarising an
arbitrary soup of segments, and the moment one wall does not quite meet its
neighbour the room silently vanishes. A room is its own object.

areaId.description: Home Assistant area id. Stored, not yet acted on (see Floor.haFloor).

double_tap_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

fillOpacity.description: 0..1. Default 0.25 â€” a room tint must not fight the walls drawn over it.

hold_action.description:
A Lovelace action (tap/hold/double_tap). Typed loosely on purpose: HA has
renamed fields over time (call-serviceâ†’perform-action, service_dataâ†’data)
and unknown fields must pass through the card untouched.

points.description: Closed polygon in canvas units; the closing edge is implicit.

stateStyles.description: Conditional fill, first match wins. `color: "rgb"` follows a light.

tap_action.description:
Lovelace actions (see {@link ActionConfig}), mirroring `Furniture`. Unlike
items/furniture, a room has no entity of its own, so there is no implicit
default tap action â€” an unconfigured room stays a non-interactive shape.
`{ action: "toggle-area-lights" }` is a card-specific convenience: toggle
every `light.*` entity in the room's `areaId` (see {@link resolveRoomAction}
in `./areas`).

tempEntity.description:
Temperature-class entity (e.g. a `sensor` with `device_class: temperature`)
that drives this room's thermal-layer tint. Unset = the room is not shaded
by the thermal layer, even when the feature is on (see
docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-thermal-layer.md).

StateStyle.description:
One conditional style rule: when the entity is in this state, look like that.

Every condition present must hold (so `above` and `below` together are a range).
A rule with no conditions always matches, which makes it a useful last entry.

State comparisons are literal on the raw state string, `unavailable` included.
The numeric ones never match a non-numeric state, so an outage simply fails
`above`/`below` rather than being treated as zero.

animation.description: How a matched {@link StateStyle} animates its element.

color.description: A CSS colour, or `"rgb"` to take the light's own `rgb_color`.

entity.description: Defaults to the element's own entity.

icon.description: mdi:... â€” beats the element's own `icon`, because it is the more specific rule.

Tracker.description:
A live position tracker driven by 1 or 2 distance sensors aimed along
orthogonal axes. The user draws a rectangular tracked area on the floor
plan and binds an HA distance entity to each axis; the card linearly
maps each sensor's `[min, max]` reading to the corresponding edge-to-edge
span of the rectangle.

With both sensors configured the card animates a pulsating triangle with
ripple rings at the resolved (x, y) inside the zone. With only one
sensor configured it animates a faint pulsating line spanning the
unknown axis (we know the target sits *somewhere* on that line).

The zone rectangle is visible only in the editor; the live card renders
only the tracked-object animation.

color.description: Marker / ripple color (CSS / hex). Falls back to the primary color.

xSensor.description: Distance sensor mapped to the X axis (rectangle's horizontal span).

ySensor.description: Distance sensor mapped to the Y axis (rectangle's vertical span).

TrackerPresence.description:
A presence / occupancy gate bound to a tracker sensor. `entity` is read as
a binary on/off state (with `invert` to flip inverted-logic sensors). When
the entity is `unavailable` / `unknown` we treat it as "not detected" â€”
better to hide a possibly-stale marker than to leave it showing during a
sensor outage.

TrackerSensor.description:
A single distance sensor mapping. `[min, max]` reading values map
linearly to the edge-to-edge span of the tracker rectangle along the
sensor's axis. `invert: true` flips the mapping (max â†’ min edge).

Optionally a `presence` entity gates the marker: when any configured
presence on the tracker reports "not detected" the animation is hidden
entirely (the zone outline still shows in the editor). This handles the
common case where a radar / mmWave device exposes both `sensor.*_distance`
and `binary_sensor.*_occupancy` as siblings â€” gating on the latter
suppresses ghost markers when the room is empty.

invert.description: Flip the mapping so that `max` corresponds to the near edge.

max.description: Sensor reading when the target is at the "far" edge.

min.description: Sensor reading when the target is at the "near" edge.

presence.description:
Optional binary entity (`binary_sensor.*`, `input_boolean`, etc.) whose
"not detected" state hides the marker animation. When unset, the marker
is never gated by presence â€” only by whether a distance reading is
available.

Wall.description: A straight wall segment in virtual coordinate space.

background.description: Canvas background color (CSS / hex). Falls back to the card background.

dayNightEntity.description:
Entity read by the day/night theme (roadmap 4a) -- normally a `sun.sun`
sensor, but any entity whose state is `above_horizon`/`below_horizon`
(or that carries a numeric `elevation` attribute) works, e.g. a template
sensor mirroring a different sun/darkness source. Defaults to `sun.sun`
(theme.ts's DEFAULT_SUN_ENTITY) when unset. Read only when
`features.dayNightTheme` is on.

defaultFloor.description: Id of the floor shown first. Falls back to the first floor.

features.description: Optional feature toggles; every flag defaults to off. See {@link FeaturesConfig}.

floors.description:
Multi-floor data. When present and non-empty this is the source of truth.
When absent, the legacy flat arrays below describe a single implicit floor
(kept for backward compatibility with hand-written configs).

grid.description: Visible editor grid spacing in virtual units (purely a visual guide).

snap.description:
Placement snap step in virtual (canvas) units. Tri-state:
- **unset** â€” placement/drag/nudge snap to the visible `grid` (the default).
- **`0`** â€” free placement (no snapping anywhere).
- **`> 0`** â€” snap to this custom step (absolute units).

The editor presents a custom step as a percentage of the grid (e.g. `50` %
of a `20` grid is stored here as `10`), but the stored value is always
absolute. Resolve with {@link resolveSnap}.

width.description: Virtual canvas size; the SVG viewBox uses these. Drawing is resolution-independent.
